# Abstract fixture identities in team/private retrieval spec

## Context

PR #11055 added the first Dockerized integration proof for #10951's team/private retrieval lane. Opus approved the PR in Cycle 1 and marked the identity naming concern as non-blocking polish:

https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/11055#issuecomment-4413629778

The spec currently uses real swarm member names as test identities:

- `neo-gpt`
- `neo-opus-ada`
- `neo-gemini-pro`

Sibling integration coverage such as `test/playwright/integration/CrossTenantIsolation.integration.spec.mjs` uses abstract fixture identities (`alice`, `bob`) instead.

Duplicate sweep before filing:

- KB ticket search for `hardcoded swarm member identities integration spec alice bob charlie TeamPrivateRetrieval follow-up` returned no relevant documents.
- `rg` across `resources/content/issues`, `resources/content/issue-archive`, and `resources/content/discussions` found no equivalent team/private retrieval fixture-identity cleanup ticket.
- Adjacent #10330 is related but not duplicate: it concerns legacy `AGENT:alice` / `AGENT:bob` / `AGENT:charlie` graph-node pollution. This ticket explicitly avoids that shape and targets plain proxy identity strings used by the integration MCP fixture.

## The Problem

Hardcoding production swarm-member identities in an integration spec couples test-fixture behavior to the current named-maintainer roster. If a named maintainer is renamed, retired, or the canonical seeded identity list changes, the spec can fail for reasons unrelated to the team/private retrieval substrate it is meant to test.

The test only needs three distinct authenticated request identities: owner, peer, and unrelated. Real maintainer names do not add coverage; they add incidental coupling.

## The Architectural Reality

Relevant surfaces:

- `test/playwright/integration/TeamPrivateRetrieval.integration.spec.mjs` from PR #11055.
- `test/playwright/integration/CrossTenantIsolation.integration.spec.mjs` sibling precedent using abstract identities.
- `test/playwright/integration/fixtures/mcpClient.mjs` sends the proxy identity through `X-PREFERRED-USERNAME`.
- `ai/mcp/server/memory-core/Server.mjs` binds proxy identities into request context and graph identity node IDs.
- #10330 warns against legacy `AGENT:alice` graph-node pollution; this cleanup should keep using plain username-style proxy identities, not `AGENT:*`.

## The Fix

Rename the #11055 integration spec identities from real swarm-member names to abstract fixture names:

- owner: `alice`
- peer: `bob`
- unrelated: `charlie`

Keep the existing owner/peer/unrelated variable names and assertions. Do not change the tested policy surface or broaden #10951.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `TeamPrivateRetrieval.integration.spec.mjs` uses abstract proxy identities for owner, peer, and unrelated users.
- [ ] The cleanup does not introduce `AGENT:alice`, `AGENT:bob`, or `AGENT:charlie` graph-node IDs.
- [ ] The spec still verifies owner-private, peer-private, shared Chroma commons, and current broad `visibility: "team"` graph semantics.
- [ ] `node --check test/playwright/integration/TeamPrivateRetrieval.integration.spec.mjs` passes.
- [ ] Docker-equipped CI or local integration run executes `TeamPrivateRetrieval.integration.spec.mjs`.

## Out of Scope

- Changing #10010/#10011 membership-qualified team authorization semantics.
- Reworking the #11055 direct-container-seeding helper.
- Renaming real swarm identities in production AgentIdentity substrate.

## Avoided Traps

- **Rejected: patching PR #11055 after approval solely for polish.** That would restart the review loop after CI is green and the reviewer explicitly marked the point non-blocking.
- **Rejected: using legacy `AGENT:alice` graph IDs.** #10330 documents why those were pollution; the intended shape is plain proxy identity usernames through the existing MCP integration fixture.

## Related

- PR #11055
- #10951
- #10010
- #10011
- #10330

- 2026-05-09T21:20:30Z @neo-opus-ada referenced in commit `c8ae509` - "test(test): seed AgentIdentity nodes for fixture identities (#11057)

Addresses PR #11060 Cycle 1 CHANGES_REQUESTED from @neo-gpt:
https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/11060#pullrequestreview-4258508174

Root cause: the abstract identity rename (`alice` / `bob` / `charlie`) broke
the second test (graph search) because `Server.bindAgentIdentity('alice')`
looks up `@alice` via `GraphService.getNode({id: '@alice'})` and finds nothing,
returning unbound. `GraphService.searchNodes()` filters by
`getAgentIdentityNodeId()`, so owner-private graph search returned empty
nodeTexts.

For real swarm members (`neo-gpt` etc.), `@neo-gpt` exists as a pre-seeded
AgentIdentity node from `seedAgentIdentities.mjs` running at MC bootstrap.
Abstract fixture identities lack that infrastructure.

Fix: seed `@alice` / `@bob` / `@charlie` AgentIdentity nodes inside
`seedGraphRecords()` BEFORE the existing test-data upsertNode calls. Mirrors
the canonical `seedAgentIdentities.mjs` upsert pattern. Cleanup is symmetric
in `cleanupGraphRecords()` — apoptosis-exempt AgentIdentity nodes require
explicit deletion to prevent fixture-identity accumulation across runs.

Avoided trap (#11057 + #10330 discipline): IDs use the canonical `@<username>`
shape (NOT the legacy `AGENT:<username>` form which is graph-node pollution
per #10330).

Empirical:
- `node --check test/playwright/integration/TeamPrivateRetrieval.integration.spec.mjs` passes
- Local Docker not available; CI integration-unified will validate the fix
- Diff: +37 / -5 (seedGraphRecords expanded with AgentIdentity seeding;
  cleanupGraphRecords accepts identityNodeIds for cleanup)

Self-Identification: @neo-opus-4-7 (Claude Opus 4.7, Claude Code)
Origin Session ID: c2912891-b459-4a03-b2af-154d5e264df1"
- 2026-05-09T21:32:36Z @tobiu referenced in commit `2005505` - "refactor(test): abstract fixture identities in team/private retrieval spec (#11057) (#11060)

* refactor(test): abstract fixture identities in team/private retrieval spec (#11057)

Renames hardcoded swarm-member identities (`neo-gpt` / `neo-opus-4-7` /
`neo-gemini-3-1-pro`) to abstract test-fixture identities (`alice` / `bob` /
`charlie`) in `test/playwright/integration/TeamPrivateRetrieval.integration.spec.mjs`,
matching the precedent established by `CrossTenantIsolation.integration.spec.mjs`
per `AGENTS.md §23 Sibling-File Lift` discipline.

Decouples the spec from production swarm membership so renames / retirements
of named maintainers don't break the team/private retrieval test for
incidental reasons unrelated to the substrate it covers.

Variable names (ownerIdentity / peerIdentity / unrelatedIdentity), assertion
paths, and tested policy surface are unchanged. Per #11057 Avoided-Trap, no
`AGENT:alice` / `AGENT:bob` / `AGENT:charlie` graph-node IDs are introduced
(would conflict with #10330 legacy-node-pollution discipline).

Empirical:
- `node --check test/playwright/integration/TeamPrivateRetrieval.integration.spec.mjs` passes
- Diff: +6 / -6 across 1 file (2 identical 3-line blocks renamed)
- Docker-equipped CI run validates Cycle 1 PR review evidence

* test(test): seed AgentIdentity nodes for fixture identities (#11057)

Addresses PR #11060 Cycle 1 CHANGES_REQUESTED from @neo-gpt:

Root cause: the abstract identity rename (`alice` / `bob` / `charlie`) broke
the second test (graph search) because `Server.bindAgentIdentity('alice')`
looks up `@alice` via `GraphService.getNode({id: '@alice'})` and finds nothing,
returning unbound. `GraphService.searchNodes()` filters by
`getAgentIdentityNodeId()`, so owner-private graph search returned empty
nodeTexts.

For real swarm members (`neo-gpt` etc.), `@neo-gpt` exists as a pre-seeded
AgentIdentity node from `seedAgentIdentities.mjs` running at MC bootstrap.
Abstract fixture identities lack that infrastructure.

Fix: seed `@alice` / `@bob` / `@charlie` AgentIdentity nodes inside
`seedGraphRecords()` BEFORE the existing test-data upsertNode calls. Mirrors
the canonical `seedAgentIdentities.mjs` upsert pattern. Cleanup is symmetric
in `cleanupGraphRecords()` — apoptosis-exempt AgentIdentity nodes require
explicit deletion to prevent fixture-identity accumulation across runs.

Avoided trap (#11057 + #10330 discipline): IDs use the canonical `@<username>`
shape (NOT the legacy `AGENT:<username>` form which is graph-node pollution
per #10330).

Empirical:
- `node --check test/playwright/integration/TeamPrivateRetrieval.integration.spec.mjs` passes

- 2026-06-05T14:41:24Z @neo-opus-grace referenced in commit `cc56bd0` - "test(memory-core): pin OIDC + team/private integration isolation to explicit private (#12527)

Two more integration isolation tests asserted cross-tenant boundaries under the (now team) default and broke deployment-wide, same class as CrossTenantIsolation. OidcAuth: alice/bob queries pin memorySharing='private' (OIDC-tenant isolation under the enforcing policy). TeamPrivateRetrieval: the 3 private-boundary raw-memory queries pin 'private'; shared-commons queries keep the default (team => shared visible); summary assertions already hold (summaries-team is additive).

Also removed pre-existing decay-prone ticket-refs from TeamPrivateRetrieval comments to satisfy the whole-file ticket-archaeology hook (#10010, #10011, #11057, #10330, #10789); behavioral descriptions preserved, refs preserved here in history. CrossTenantIsolation fix already CI-verified green; this completes the integration suite for the team default.

Authored by neo-opus-grace (Claude Opus)."
- 2026-06-05T16:09:47Z @tobiu referenced in commit `352344b` - "feat(ai): default memory-sharing to team for deployment-wide raw reads (#12532)

* feat(ai): default memory-sharing to team for deployment-wide raw reads (#12527)

Raw memory becomes a team commons per operator direction: every maintainer reads every maintainer's raw memories (transparent swarm introspection).

- memorySharing.defaultPolicy: legacy -> team
- queryMemories + listMemories team branch: tenantScope=null (deployment-wide, no restrictive userId filter); the legacy-only post-filter does not run for team
- private/legacy unchanged (isolation stays opt-in); summaries already deployment-wide under team (#12470)
- Tenant-isolation spec: team tests assert cross-author visibility, legacy/private pinned via explicit policy; 16/16 green under team default

* fix(memory-core): listMemories memorySharing override for team-read symmetry (#12527)

listMemories read its policy only from the ambient AiConfig defaultPolicy, unlike queryMemories which already accepts a memorySharing override. Under the new team default this left listMemories untestable without mutating the shared AiConfig singleton (ADR-0019 safety-critical antipattern) and asymmetric with queryMemories.

Add the optional memorySharing param (mirrors queryMemories) so team / deployment-wide reads are explicit and unit-testable; existing callers are unchanged (param defaults to the ambient policy).

config.template.spec: assert defaultPolicy now resolves to team. MemoryService.TenantIsolation.spec: drive listMemories team reads via the explicit override instead of relying on (or mutating) the ambient singleton. Verified: target specs 42 passed; both touched specs green in isolation.

Authored by neo-opus-grace (Claude Opus).

* test(memory-core): cross-tenant integration covers team-default + private isolation (#12527)

The MC cross-tenant isolation integration test asserted isolation under the (now team) default and broke once raw reads went deployment-wide. Restructured to the split-coverage shape: pin the leak guard to explicit memorySharing='private' (the contract multi-tenant forks rely on), and add a positive team-default assertion (alice reads bob's same-session record, deployment-wide — the operator PRIO-1 behavior).

query_raw_memories already threads memorySharing (toolService binds it straight to queryMemories; openapi exposes it), so this is test-only. Verified: syntax-checked; logic mirrors the unit private/team coverage. Docker integration runs on CI.

Authored by neo-opus-grace (Claude Opus).

* test(memory-core): pin OIDC + team/private integration isolation to explicit private (#12527)

Two more integration isolation tests asserted cross-tenant boundaries under the (now team) default and broke deployment-wide, same class as CrossTenantIsolation. OidcAuth: alice/bob queries pin memorySharing='private' (OIDC-tenant isolation under the enforcing policy). TeamPrivateRetrieval: the 3 private-boundary raw-memory queries pin 'private'; shared-commons queries keep the default (team => shared visible); summary assertions already hold (summaries-team is additive).

Also removed pre-existing decay-prone ticket-refs from TeamPrivateRetrieval comments to satisfy the whole-file ticket-archaeology hook (#10010, #10011, #11057, #10330, #10789); behavioral descriptions preserved, refs preserved here in history. CrossTenantIsolation fix already CI-verified green; this completes the integration suite for the team default.

Authored by neo-opus-grace (Claude Opus).
